entries.title: EXPLANATION ON THE BOOK OF ESTHER 11 on esther 8:3
entries.excerpt: The fact that Esther falls at the feet of the king and entreats him for the salvation of the people plainly symbolizes the holy church that humbly implores the almighty Lord every day for the rescue of her children through the faith and the mystery of the incarnation of the only begotten Son, so that the arrogance of her enemies may be restrained by the (Lord's) grace, and the innocence of the faithful may be freed from the clutches of the (wicked).

entries.title: Commentary on Esther on esther 8:3
entries.excerpt: So when Esther falls at the feet of the king and beseeches him for the sake of her people, this must refer to the fact that every day the holy Church humbly petitions the Lord Almighty—through the faith and mysteries of the incarnate, only-born Son of God—to free her children so that through his grace, the arrogance of the enemy might be crushed and the innocence of the faithful be freed from their hands.
